---
title: "live_distributions"
author: "Sahifa"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
source("../scripts/ggprob.R")
```

```{r}
# d function gives you the prob at a point x
# Example: Flip a coin 10 times

# prob of getting 4 heads

dbinom(4, 10, 1/2)
choose(10, 4)*(1/2)^4 * (1/2)^6

sum(dbinom(0:10, 10, 1/2))
```


```{r}
# p function by default gives you the probability to the left of a point q.

# find the prob of getting at most 4 = P(x<= 4)

pbinom(4, 10, 1/2)

sum(dbinom(0:4, 10, 1/2))

# find the prob of getting atleast 4 = P(x > 3) or P(X ≥ 4)

1 - pbinom(3, 10, 1/2)
pbinom(3, 10, 1/2, lower.tail = F)

sum(dbinom(4:10, 10, 1/2))


1 - pbinom(4, 10, 1/2) # this will give you the wrong prob
```

```{r}
# q function by default gives you the point that covers a certain prob to the left, is the inverse of the p function

# median (and the mean since this is a symmetric binomial distribution) of the 10 coin flips

qbinom(0.5, 10, 1/2)
qbinom(0.5, 10, 1/2, lower.tail = F)

pbinom(4, 10, 1/2)
pbinom(5, 10, 1/2)
pbinom(4, 10, 1/2, lower.tail = F)
sum(dbinom(0:5, 10, 1/2))
sum(dbinom(5:10, 10, 1/2))

qbinom(pbinom(5, 10, 1/2), 10, 1/2)

# 90th percentile, 75th percentile, 25th percentile
```

```{r}
# r function generates a sample of size 'n'

rbinom(10, 10, 1/2)
```


```{r}
gbinom(2000, 0.1)
gbinom(2000, 0.4)
```


# Normal Distribution

- N(10, 0.2)

```{r}
dnorm(2.67, 10, 0.2)
dnorm(2.671, 10, 0.2)
dnorm(2.6712, 10, 0.2)

pnorm(2.6, 10, 0.7)
```
